heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 225 and it happened on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 18 July 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 1st since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 03 July 2026 and has lasted 23 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C) on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwave right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 28 June 2026, there have been only 68 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (211°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 5.2°C) and outside temperature (down 3.9°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 13%).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1782 m), feels like temperature (up 5.9°C), outside temperature (up 4.4°C), and outside humidity (down 11%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 1500.4m lower than yesterday, 1782.0m lower than the past week, 1364.9m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1666m minimum and 6280m maximum, with an average of 2450m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4230m and 3810m.
cloud levels.summary: The clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mostly high temperatures. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and very little to no wind will be present. Showers, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and the light is absent. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
